resources.string: Select a date to calculate Tattvas
Use GPS or search to add locations
Are you sure you want to delete \"%1$s\"?
This will delete all saved locations except București. This action cannot be undone.
Type at least 2 characters to search
Try a different search term\nor use (+) button to add manually
Tap to request your current location

The first Nakshatra of the zodiac, Ashwini represents beginnings, healing and vital energy. Under the protection of the divine twins Ashwini Kumara, the celestial healers, this Nakshatra brings swiftness, spontaneity and healing power.
Bharani is the Nakshatra of birth and transformation, ruled by Yama, the god of death. It represents the cycle of life, fertility and responsibility. Its energy is profound and transformative.
Krittika, the purifying flame, is ruled by Agni, the god of fire. It symbolizes the burning of impurities and mental clarity. It brings powerful energy of transformation and illumination.
Rohini is considered the most favorable Nakshatra for growth and abundance. Under the rule of the Moon, it represents fertility, creativity and material beauty.
Mrigashira, the deer\'s head, represents seeking and exploration. Ruled by Mars and associated with Soma, it brings curiosity, sensitivity and the desire to discover truth.
Ardra is the Nakshatra of storms and radical transformation. Under the protection of Rudra (Shiva), it brings intense changes, purification through suffering and spiritual rebirth.
Punarvasu means \'return of the light\'. Ruled by Jupiter and protected by Aditi, mother of the gods, it brings optimism, renewal and the possibility of a new beginning.
Pushya is considered the most auspicious and nourishing Nakshatra. Ruled by Saturn and protected by Brihaspati, it brings spiritual growth, nourishment and protection.
Ashlesha, the cosmic serpent, is ruled by Mercury and associated with Naga. It represents kundalini power, deep intuition and hidden wisdom, but also the possibility of manipulation.
Magha represents the ancestral throne and royal power. Ruled by Ketu and protected by Pitri (ancestors), it brings honor, respect for tradition and connection with spiritual roots.
Purva Phalguni is the Nakshatra of pleasure, relaxation and artistic creativity. Ruled by Venus and protected by Bhaga, the god of welfare, it brings joy, romance and appreciation for beauty.
Uttara Phalguni represents beneficial partnerships and mutual support. Ruled by the Sun and protected by Aryaman, the god of friendship, it brings loyalty, generosity and stability in relationships.
Hasta, the hand, symbolizes skill, precision and the ability to manifest. Ruled by the Moon and protected by Savitar, the sun god, it brings practical talent and the ability to create.
Chitra is the Nakshatra of the divine jewel and perfect beauty. Ruled by Mars and protected by Tvashtar, the cosmic architect, it brings creativity, aesthetics and the desire for perfection.
Swati, the leaf carried by the wind, represents independence and adaptability. Ruled by Rahu and protected by Vayu, the wind god, it brings freedom, flexibility and the desire for change.
Vishakha is the Nakshatra of determination and ambition. Ruled by Jupiter and protected by Indra and Agni, it brings concentration power, perseverance and the ability to achieve great objectives.
Anuradha represents devoted friendship and spiritual collaboration. Ruled by Saturn and protected by Mitra, the god of friendship, it brings loyalty, devotion and the ability to work in harmony.
Jyeshtha is the Nakshatra of seniority and power. Ruled by Mercury and protected by Indra, the king of gods, it brings authority, protection and the responsibility of leadership.

Mula represents the root and deep investigation. Ruled by Ketu and protected by Nirriti, the goddess of destruction, it brings radical transformation, the search for truth and the elimination of what no longer serves.
Purva Ashadha is the Nakshatra of invincibility and optimism. Ruled by Venus and protected by Apah, the water goddess, it brings confidence, conquering energy and the capacity for purification.
Uttara Ashadha represents final victory and permanent achievement. Ruled by the Sun and protected by Vishvadeva, it brings lasting success, recognition and universal authority.

Shravana is the Nakshatra of listening and learning. Ruled by the Moon and protected by Vishnu, the preserver, it brings wisdom through listening, spiritual connection and the ability to receive knowledge.

Dhanishta represents prosperity and cosmic rhythm. Ruled by Mars and protected by Vasus, the gods of abundance, it brings material success, musicality and the ability to work in rhythm with the universe.
Shatabhisha, the hundred healers, is ruled by Rahu and protected by Varuna, the god of cosmic waters. It brings mysterious healing, deep intuition and the ability to discover hidden secrets.

Purva Bhadrapada represents the burning of karma and intense spiritual transformation. Ruled by Jupiter and protected by Aja Ekapada, it brings intensity, spiritual passion and the desire for transcendence.

Uttara Bhadrapada is the Nakshatra of depth and cosmic wisdom. Ruled by Saturn and protected by Ahirbudhnya, the serpent of the depths, it brings spiritual stability, profound wisdom and the ability to sustain the world.

Revati, the last Nakshatra, represents the final journey and divine protection. Ruled by Mercury and protected by Pushan, the god of journeys, it brings spiritual nourishment, completion and guidance to destination.
